title: ferolia curatellifolia
intro: Ferolia curatellifolia, also known as the "Bitter Apple" or "Manchineel," is a small, woody tree or shrub native to the Caribbean and Central America. Its medicinal properties include the presence of various compounds, such as curatellone, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ferolia curatellifolia are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain, and promoting overall well-being. Ferolia curatellifolia is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water, although it is essential to exercise caution due to its toxic sap.
